text:
It was raining heavily, with sharp gusts shaking the old oak tree in the backyard. The branches swayed violently, threatening to tear themselves away at any moment.

A young girl named Lily leaned playfully against one of the branches. "You're shaking like a bowl of thunder!"

Her father chuckled as he came into view. "What has you got there, dear?" he asked, wiping the rain off his wet beard.

"Lovely little storm creature!" Lily declared with a giggle.

He picked her up gently and set her onto another branch, then looked at the rain-soaked tree. "'What kind of creature am I? A leaf?'"

He laughed. "'No, a bear. A wise old bear.'"

She hugged her shoulder just above her elbow. "I'll call you Mr. Bear. I bet you've seen lightning strikes before."

He glanced up at the darkening sky and smiled. "Probably the hundreds of times since I've lived here. But you're
